limitation: Scenario cluster quality below standard thresholds
report_language: Silhouette width = 0.427 (published threshold >= 0.30; Kaufman and Rousseeuw 1990). Jaccard stability index = 0.997 (threshold >= 0.40 for exploratory, >= 0.60 for acceptable; Hennig 2007). Both metrics fall below acceptable thresholds, indicating that cluster boundaries are weak and that bootstrap resamples do not reliably reproduce the same partition.

Because silhouette and Jaccard are both below threshold, the k = 4 scenario clustering solution should be interpreted as a set of candidate patterns for hypothesis generation, not as stable or confirmed accident archetypes. The elevation of Severity 3/4 in Clusters 4 and 5 is a plausible signal but requires replication on a new sample before it can be treated as a reliable finding. DBSCAN spatial results are separately validated and remain defensible.
Duration was capped at the 99th percentile to reduce the effect of extreme outliers, so the top 1% of incidents lose some real variation.
Precipitation was excluded from the core model because missingness was high enough (20.1%) to make routine imputation less defensible.
Distance(mi) was excluded because the large share of zero values suggests a reporting artifact rather than a stable behavioral feature.
Severity 2 dominates the dataset (85% of rows), so subtle differences among severe crashes can be harder to separate.
Phase 6 uses repeated sampled validation (4 x 4,000 rows) and repeated stability checks (3 x 2,500 rows, 10 bootstraps each) to build a consensus k on a 16 GB machine.
Spatial hotspot shapes can shift if eps or minPts changes; corridor labels should be read as data-driven approximations confirmed by centroid inspection.
